headline: Paddy Farmers Protest RM1,200 Per Tonne Price Cap
context: BERNAS's paddy procurement price ceiling of RM1,200 per tonne sparked protests among 170,000 rice farmers in Kedah and Perlis in February 2026, who demanded RM1,500 to cover rising input costs.

cards.big: 170,000 paddy farmers earn RM1,100 monthly average — BERNAS profits RM380 million while farmers can't cover costs
cards.sub: Rice production costs rose 28% since 2022 but BERNAS farmgate price increased only 9% in same period

cards.big: Malaysia imports 34% of rice at RM2,800 per tonne while paying domestic farmers only RM1,200 — a RM1,600 gap
cards.sub: Self-sufficiency ratio fell from 72% to 63% in 5 years as farmers abandon rice for more profitable crops

cards.big: Kedah paddy farmers average age 59 — young Malaysians refuse to enter farming at current income levels
cards.sub: Rice farming communities in MADA irrigation area show 28% poverty rate — highest among agricultural sectors

cards.big: BERNAS holds monopoly on rice imports since 1996 privatisation — guaranteed margins regardless of farmer prices
cards.sub: Government renewed BERNAS concession in 2021 for 10 years without competitive tender or transparency review

cards.big: A rice monopoly that profits RM380 million while the farmers who grow the rice live in poverty is not food security — it's extraction
Should BERNAS's rice import monopoly be revoked and replaced with an open licensing system to improve farmer bargaining power?
